description:
Goal: wire backend consumers to the closed Compiler2 artifact contract produced by fz-rh2.8.4. Signal: interpreter, JIT, and AOT adapters consume EmissionReadyProgram(root) mechanically and never rediscover reachability, dispatch, return lanes, callable entries, extern marshal classes, or type flow.

Blocked on fz-rh2.8.4: backend adapter work resumes only after Compiler2 owns and documents the MaterializedProgram -> AbiReadyProgram -> EmissionReadyProgram ladder. This ticket should consume that settled surface, not decide the data model.

Change:
- Move interpreter/JIT/AOT adapter entry points to consume EmissionReadyProgram and its ABI/callable/effect/extern facts.
- Remove backend fallback paths that ask semantic maps, type inference, old planner, or old codegen fact derivation to fill missing artifact data.
- Treat unsupported or missing closed facts as fatal artifact/adapter errors; telemetry carries raw ids/facts/structures, not formatted summaries.

Why:
- Backend work should lower settled facts, not decide reachability, dispatch, return lanes, callable entries, extern marshalling, or type flow.

Checks:
- Backend attempts to ask semantic/type/callee/reachability questions after the artifact boundary are fatal.
- Quicksort and Enum.reduce preserve the expected frontier, callable-entry, return ABI, and allocation/effect contracts through telemetry plus runtime/artifact assertions.
- Variadic extern fixtures consume frozen marshal facts from the artifact surface.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive ABI and emission from closed facts
